source: R datasets::airquality — NYSDEC 1973 daily air quality; 153 daily observations, ozone (ppb), solar radiation, wind, temperature, May–September
notes: Control study: low wind consistently predicts high ozone (>50 ppb) in both summer and shoulder-season strata. Season confounds base rates — summer is both hotter and has more calm days — but does not reverse the wind-ozone direction. Good environmental/meteorological domain addition.
